Document:
# Uniform Code of Military Justice
These are sets of laws and rules used by GOVFOR/OPFOR and all relevant military factions.
### Basic Principles - All personnel are required to both enforce the law and abide by it. - MP/Law Enforcement must investigate reported crimes; however, if an investigation determines the offence to be minor, it may be disregarded at their discretion. - MP/Law Enforcement breaking the law in any capacity cannot be ignored. - Charges are not to be applied to those who use force on others when defending themselves from illegal harm, so long as they defend themselves with proportional force. This also extends to the defense of others if they believe the others are in lethal danger. - No person may serve more than thirty minutes in the brig for the total accumulation of their crimes at time of sentencing, unless on permanent confinement. - At no point is an execution ever to be sanctioned nor is it legal.
## Categories - [textlink="Criminal Code" link="AU14UCMJCriminalCode"] - [textlink="Right To Appeal" link="AU14UCMJRightToAppeal"] - [textlink="Non-Judicial Punishments" link="AU14UCMJNJP"] - [textlink="Arresting Procedures" link="AU14UCMJArrestingProcedure"] - [textlink="Law Rank Structure" link="AU14UCMJLawRankStructure"] - [textlink="Definitions and Glossary" link="AU14UCMJDefinitionsandGlossary"] - [textlink="Geneva Convention" link="AU14UCMJGENCON"]
